title: podman generate systemd fails with "no create command found"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

Rather a question. I wanted to post on the mailing list but moderator did not approve subscription so far. So I'm trying here.

**Description**

I'm using podman 3.2.2. on Ubuntu 20.04 and have a Kubernetes pod manifest on which I ran `podman play kube` (all of the follwing is in root mode). I then wanted to generate systemd files from it and ran `podman generate systemd --files --name --new PODNAME` which failed with the error message `Error: cannot use --new on pod "POD_HASH": no create command found`.

This [article](https://www.jwillikers.com/a-podman-pod-as-a-systemd-service) seems to suggest that it would be possible to use `generate systemd --new` after creating a pod with `play kube`. Not sure if this is true. How would one supply a create command or does this functionality not work with pods created from a Kubernetes manifest?

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Take the following manifest file:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: docker-registry-proxy
spec:
  containers:
  - name: registry-proxy
    image: ghcr.io/rpardini/docker-registry-proxy:0.6.2
    env:
    - name: ENABLE_MANIFEST_CACHE
      value: "true"
    - name: CACHE_MAX_SIZE
      value: "10g"
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /docker_mirror_cache
      name: mirror-cache
    - mountPath: /ca
      name: ca
    ports:
    - containerPort: 3128
      hostPort: 3128
  volumes:
  - name: mirror-cache
    hostPath:
      path: /opt/docker-registry-proxy/cache
  - name: ca
    persistentVolumeClaim:
      claimName: docker-registry-proxy-ca
```

2. Run `podman play kube`

3. Run `podman generate systemd --files --name --new PODNAME`

**Describe the results you received:**
`Error: cannot use --new on pod "POD_HASH": no create command found`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Command would generate systemd service files which create containers anew upon start.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
N/A

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.2
API Version:  3.2.2
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

    version: |-
      crun version 0.20.1.5-925d-dirty
      commit: 0d42f1109fd73548f44b01b3e84d04a279e99d2e
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: true
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false

  serviceIsRemote: false

```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @nebulon42!

I think the article is wrong.  `podman generate systemd` requires the "create command" to be set for container/pod. The create command is, in fact, just the exact command (e.g., "podman run -d fedora top").  

>  How would one supply a create command or does this functionality not work with pods created from a Kubernetes manifest?

Since the create command is not set during "play kube", I fear this has never worked.  `podman generate systemd` only works on containers/pods created on the CLI:
```
$ podman pod create --name=testpod
$ podman create --pod=testpod --name=testcontainer fedora top
$ podman generate systemd --new testpod
```

I am going to close the issue, since `play kube` is something we cannot support.
